quotes.topic: exercise approach: notes then chords then tunes
quotes.verbatim_quote: Try playing these next exercises first using any notes, then maybe just notes from one chord, and

!f vou need another challenge try moving all of the exercises over and starting them one beat ear-
ly, ov try one beat late, so that the polyrhythm goes over the 4/4 bar line.
Take a group of notes and try playing them over two beats. For example try this five-note grouping:

Expand and try playing these five notes over three beats, and then over four beats. Next try playing i
the same five notes over 1 1/2 beats by contracting them.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Dual expansion/contraction operations on fixed cell.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Try taking a three-note melody like,

and play it in one beat, then two beats, three beats, and four beats. Try playing the notes in 1 1/2
beats, or play these notes three times in two beats
quotes.why_load_bearing: Generalizes contraction/expansion to any melodic fragment.

quotes.verbatim_quote: This concept of expansion or contraction is another way to perceive odd groupings of notes.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Cognitive reframing strategy for odd tuplets.
